incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 1: doesn't recognize that the scale won't fit the data

steps.text: Let's think about this together. Ben collected 70 toys. Scale of 1 would need 70 lines on your graph. That's way too many to fit.

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 2: doesn't recognize that the scale won't fit the data

steps.text: You're working on it. Scale of 2 would need 35 lines to reach 70. That's still too many lines to fit on one graph.

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 5: doesn't recognize that the scale won't fit the data

steps.text: You're getting there. The key is that Scale of 5 would need 14 lines to show 70. That's better than 1 or 2, but still won't fit.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Scale of 10 highlights and pulses to show it's the correct choice

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Ben collected 70 toys, the highest number. Scale of 10 only needs 8 lines to reach 70. Scales of 1, 2, and 5 all need too many lines. Scale of 10 fits best.
